Reviewer's guide (collapsed on small PRs) ## Reviewer's Guide This PR replaces a hard-coded CurrentBar threshold with a framework-backed BarsRequiredToTrade guard and initializes BarsRequiredToTrade to 20 during lifecycle configuration to prevent out-of-range bar index access in indicator calculations. #### Flow diagram for OnBarUpdate bar-guard logic using BarsRequiredToTrade ```mermaid flowchart TD A[OnBarUpdate] --> B{BarsInProgress == 0} B -- no --> C[return] B -- yes --> D{CurrentBar < BarsRequiredToTrade} D -- yes --> C D -- no --> E[Process indicators and strategy logic] ``` ### File-Level Changes | Change | Details | Files | | ------ | ------- | ----- | | Replace magic-number CurrentBar guard with BarsRequiredToTrade-based safety check in bar update logic. |
  • Guard bar-update processing with CurrentBar < BarsRequiredToTrade instead of a hard-coded CurrentBar < 5 check.
  • Document rationale in-line, referencing P0 criticality and correctness-by-construction intent.
| `src/V12_002.BarUpdate.cs` | | Initialize BarsRequiredToTrade to cover indicator lookback requirements during configure state. |
  • Set BarsRequiredToTrade = 20 when configuring strategy lifecycle to match ATR(14) and EMA(200) lookback needs.
  • Annotate BarsRequiredToTrade assignment with P0 criticality and correctness-by-construction comment for future reviewers.
| `src/V12_002.Lifecycle.cs` |
---
Tips and commands #### Interacting with Sourcery - **Trigger a new review:** Comment `@sourcery-ai review` on the pull request. - **Continue discussions:** Reply directly to Sourcery's review comments. - **Generate a GitHub issue from a review comment:** Ask Sourcery to create an issue from a review comment by replying to it. You can also reply to a review comment with `@sourcery-ai issue` to create an issue from it. - **Generate a pull request title:** Write `@sourcery-ai` anywhere in the pull request title to generate a title at any time. You can also comment `@sourcery-ai title` on the pull request to (re-)generate the title at any time. - **Generate a pull request summary:** Write `@sourcery-ai summary` anywhere in the pull request body to generate a PR summary at any time exactly where you want it. You can also comment `@sourcery-ai summary` on the pull request to (re-)generate the summary at any time. - **Generate reviewer's guide:** Comment `@sourcery-ai guide` on the pull request to (re-)generate the reviewer's guide at any time. - **Resolve all Sourcery comments:** Comment `@sourcery-ai resolve` on the pull request to resolve all Sourcery comments. Useful if you've already addressed all the comments and don't want to see them anymore. - **Dismiss all Sourcery reviews:** Comment `@sourcery-ai dismiss` on the pull request to dismiss all existing Sourcery reviews. Especially useful if you want to start fresh with a new review - don't forget to comment `@sourcery-ai review` to trigger a new review! #### Customizing Your Experience Access your [dashboard](https://app.sourcery.ai) to: - Enable or disable review features such as the Sourcery-generated pull request summary, the reviewer's guide, and others. - Change the review language. - Add, remove or edit custom review instructions. - Adjust other review settings. #### Getting Help - [Contact our support team](mailto:support@sourcery.ai) for questions or feedback. - Visit our [documentation](https://docs.sourcery.ai) for detailed guides and information. - Keep in touch with the Sourcery team by following us on [X/Twitter](https://x.com/SourceryAI), [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/company/sourcery-ai/) or [GitHub](https://github.com/sourcery-ai).

Code Review Ô£à Approved 1 resolved / 1 findings Increases `BarsRequiredToTrade` to 200 to ensure indicator safety and replaces hard-coded index guards in `OnBarUpdate`. These changes resolve the insufficient initial bar threshold finding.
Ô£à 1 resolved
Ô£à Bug: BarsRequiredToTrade=20 insufficient for EMA(200) > ­ƒôä src/V12_002.Lifecycle.cs:367-368 > The PR description states this change "covers ATR 14 + EMA 200 requirements," but `BarsRequiredToTrade = 20` only guarantees 20 bars exist before processing. The EMA(200) indicator needs ~200 bars to produce meaningful values. While NinjaTrader won't throw an index-out-of-range (EMA returns partial results with fewer bars), the strategy will trade on unreliable EMA(200) signals for the first ~180 bars. > > If the intent is crash prevention only, the value works but the comment is misleading. If the intent is correctness of indicator values (as the "Jane Street: Correctness by construction" comment implies), the value should be at least 200.

reviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request replaces magic numbers with `BarsRequiredToTrade` when checking if the current bar is sufficient for trading. However, setting `BarsRequiredToTrade = 20` is insufficient because the `EMA 200` indicator requires at least 200 bars to calculate. Setting it to 20 will cause an `IndexOutOfRangeException` or invalid values when accessing `ema200[0]` in `OnBarUpdate`. It is recommended to increase `BarsRequiredToTrade` to at least 200 to ensure correctness and prevent runtime exceptions.

## Review Summary

This PR addresses index safety by replacing a magic number with `BarsRequiredToTrade`, which is a good practice for correctness by construction. However, there is a critical issue that blocks merge:

### Critical Issue (Must Fix)
- **BarsRequiredToTrade Value**: Set to 20 but EMA 200 requires 200 bars. This will cause the strategy to execute with uninitialized indicator values, leading to incorrect trading logic.

### Change Assessment
- Ô£à Approach is correct (using framework-enforced BarsRequiredToTrade)
- ÔØî Value calculation is incorrect (20 vs 200 required)

The fix is straightforward: change the value from 20 to 200 to match the largest indicator period (EMA 200).

---
You can now have the agent implement changes and create commits directly on your pull request's source branch. Simply comment with /q followed by your request in natural language to ask the agent to make changes.

Hey - I've left some high level feedback:

- Consider replacing the hard-coded `BarsRequiredToTrade = 20` with a named constant or calculation derived from the actual indicator lookback requirements (e.g., `Math.Max(atrPeriod, emaPeriod))` to avoid future drift if those periods change.
- It may be safer to set `BarsRequiredToTrade` explicitly within the relevant `OnStateChange` state (e.g., `State.Configure`) to make the lifecycle intent clearer and reduce the chance of it being moved or called from an unexpected state in future changes.
- The in-line comments with labels like `P0 CRITICAL` and `Jane Street: Correctness by construction` are process-specific; consider simplifying them to focus on what the guard does and why the particular value is chosen to keep the codebase easier to maintain for future readers.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

~~~markdown
Please address the comments from this code review:

## Overall Comments
- Consider replacing the hard-coded `BarsRequiredToTrade = 20` with a named constant or calculation derived from the actual indicator lookback requirements (e.g., `Math.Max(atrPeriod, emaPeriod))` to avoid future drift if those periods change.
- It may be safer to set `BarsRequiredToTrade` explicitly within the relevant `OnStateChange` state (e.g., `State.Configure`) to make the lifecycle intent clearer and reduce the chance of it being moved or called from an unexpected state in future changes.
- The in-line comments with labels like `P0 CRITICAL` and `Jane Street: Correctness by construction` are process-specific; consider simplifying them to focus on what the guard does and why the particular value is chosen to keep the codebase easier to maintain for future readers.
~~~

</details>

***

<summary>Sourcery is free for open source - if you like our reviews please consider sharing them Ô£¿</summary>

<sub>
Help me be more useful! Please click ­ƒæì or ­ƒæÄ on each comment and I'll use the feedback to improve your reviews.
</sub>

### Pull Request Overview

While this PR correctly identifies the need for dynamic index safety using `BarsRequiredToTrade`, there are two critical blockers that should prevent merging:

1. **Acceptance Criteria Gap**: The PR description states the change is intended to support an EMA 200, yet the code sets `BarsRequiredToTrade` to 20. This will still result in `IndexOutOfRangeException` when the EMA 200 indicator is accessed, as it requires 200 bars of historical data.
2. **Architectural Risk**: The safety guard in `OnBarUpdate` is placed too high, blocking non-trading infrastructure logic such as IPC command processing and heartbeats. On higher timeframes, this leaves the strategy unresponsive to external 'Flatten' or emergency commands for several hours during the warmup phase.

Additionally, the coverage report is currently empty, meaning these safety-critical changes are not verified by automated tests.

#### About this PR
- The mismatch between the 20-bar implementation and the EMA 200 requirement is a significant risk. If an EMA 200 is used, the strategy will crash upon initialization as soon as it attempts to access the indicator with only 20 bars available.



#### Test suggestions
- [ ] Verify OnBarUpdate exits immediately when CurrentBar is less than BarsRequiredToTrade (20).
- [ ] Verify OnBarUpdate proceeds with logic execution when CurrentBar is 20 or greater.
- [ ] Verify BarsRequiredToTrade is initialized to exactly 20 during the OnStateChangeConfigure phase.

<details>
<summary>Prompt proposal for missing tests</summary>

```
Consider implementing these tests if applicable:
1. Verify OnBarUpdate exits immediately when CurrentBar is less than BarsRequiredToTrade (20).
2. Verify OnBarUpdate proceeds with logic execution when CurrentBar is 20 or greater.
3. Verify BarsRequiredToTrade is initialized to exactly 20 during the OnStateChangeConfigure phase.
```

</details>



##

`299-302`: _ÔÜá´©Å Potential issue_ | _­ƒö┤ Critical_ | _ÔÜí Quick win_ **Critical: Null check on non-nullable struct is dead code.** `TrailUpdateSignal` is a `struct` (line 56), and the parameter `update` is not nullable. The check `if (update is null)` will **always** be false, and the `ArgumentNullException` is unreachable. This is the same critical issue as in `BroadcastTradeSignal`. Apply the same fix as suggested for `BroadcastTradeSignal`: either remove the null check, validate required fields, or make the parameter nullable if needed. As per coding guidelines, C# 8.0 / .NET Framework 4.8 (NinjaTrader 8) is the language and runtime, where non-nullable value types cannot be null.
